steps.title: Understanding the solution requirement
steps.content: A point is a solution to the system if it satisfies both inequalities simultaneously. Since the choices are simple integers, backsolving is the fastest approach.

steps.title: Test choices B and C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (0, 16):
Substitute into the first inequality: $16 \leq 0 + 15 = 15$ is false, since $16 > 15$. Fails.

Backsolving — testing choice C (0, -16):
Substitute into the first inequality: $-16 \leq 0 + 15 = 15$ is true. Now check the second: $-16 \geq -4(0) - 15 = -15$ is false, since $-16 \not\geq -15$. Fails.

steps.title: Test choice D and confirm
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice D (7, 0):
  • First inequality: $0 \leq 7 + 15 = 22$ is true
  • Second inequality: $0 \geq -4(7) - 15 = -28 - 15 = -43$ is true, since $0 > -43$
Both inequalities are satisfied. That matches choice D — D is correct.
